title: Capitularina
taxonomy: Capitularina is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus is primarily found in South America, particularly in the tropical and subtropical regions of Brazil, Argentina, and Paraguay. Capitularina species are characterized by their small to medium-sized flowers, which are usually yellow or white in color. The plants are known to grow as annuals or perennials, depending on the specific species and environmental conditions. Capitularina species are often used in traditional medicine and have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
uses: Capitularina is known to have been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various ailments. It has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In folk medicine, it has been used to treat wounds and infections. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. Its medicinal uses are primarily based on anecdotal evidence and traditional practices.
